title: Mechanical Maintenance Technician
description:
About Aster

Aster Chemicals and Energy (Aster) is a leading provider of chemical and energy solutions in Singapore and Southeast Asia. Aster is a joint venture led by Chandra Asri in partnership with Glencore Group.

Building on the foundations of Singapore’s first oil refinery, our facilities on Pulau Bukom and Jurong Island serve as the nation’s hub for integrated refining and petrochemical production. Aster Bukom operates a 237,000 barrels per day refinery and a 1.1 million tonne per year ethylene cracker, while Aster Jurong Island produces essential petrochemicals that support industries across the region.


With over 60 years of heritage, Aster is shaping the future by driving progress and creating new opportunities for our people. From advancing energy solutions to driving petrochemical innovation, we welcome you to join us in our journey that reflects our commitment to operational excellence in an evolving industry landscape.


Together, we can achieve more — come be a part of it.

About the Job

We are seeking a Mechanical Maintenance Technician to support day-to-day maintenance of rotating and static equipment. This role ensures plant reliability, safe execution of field activities, and compliance with HSSE requirements while supporting engineers and contractors in delivering maintenance goals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Carry out maintenance, repair, and servicing of rotating and static equipment such as pumps, compressors, turbines, pipelines, valves, vessels, tanks, hydraulic systems, and lifting equipment.
  • Supervise contractors during field activities (e.g. lifting, welding, alignment, flange tightening) to ensure safe and quality work.
  • Support engineers in troubleshooting bad actor equipment and updating repair status in GSAP.
  • Maintain accurate equipment records, technical drawings, and documentation.
  • Use GSAP to manage work requests, preventive/corrective maintenance, and job histories.
  • Ensure compliance with site HSSE standards, participate in risk assessments, and perform duties as Permit Applicant and Permit Verifier.
  • Guide and mentor mechanical personnel and contractors to meet maintenance targets (HSSE, KPIs, budgets, schedules).
  • Collaborate with multi-discipline teams, stakeholders, and vendors to resolve issues and improve reliability.
  • Drive personal accountability and continuous improvement in maintenance performance.

Job Requirements

  • Minimum NITEC certification or equivalent in Mechanical or related discipline.
  • 3–5 years’ relevant experience in construction or maintenance within oil & gas, chemical, or petrochemical industries.
  • Proven ability to diagnose and troubleshoot equipment failures.
  • Familiarity with SAP (GSAP) Maintenance Module preferred.
  • Strong fieldwork experience with contractors and stakeholder engagement.
  • Good communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Self-motivated team player, able to work independently when required.

Additional Information

  • Work location: Jurong Island
  • Candidates must be comfortable with on-site work, including turnaround and shutdown periods.
  • We thank all applicants for their interest. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

postedCompany.description: Aster Chemicals and Energy is a leading provider of chemical and energy solutions in Singapore and in the Asia region. Building on the foundations of Singapore’s first oil refinery, Aster has a fully integrated refinery and petrochemical manufacturing operation on Pulau Bukom, and downstream chemical assets on Jurong Island.
